channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Georgia governor expected to sign 'Riley Gaines Act,' which bans trans athletes from entering girls' sports
item.description: The Georgia legislature passed a bill Monday to prohibit biological boys from participating in girls' sports and using girls' spaces, including restrooms and locker rooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chuck Schumer says he's taking the fight over federal spending to Trump country
item.description: Senate Democratic Leader Chuck Schumer is taking the fight over Medicaid cuts deep into Republican territory, visiting nursing homes in GOP-held districts to warn of what Democrats call devastating consequences if proposed federal rollbacks go through.
item.dc:creator: Michael Balsamo, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: A Senate vote to reverse Trump's tariffs on Canada is testing Republican support
item.description: With President Donald Trump’s so-called “Liberation Day” of tariff implementation fast approaching, Senate Democrats are putting Republican support for some of those plans to the test by forcing a vote to nullify the emergency declaration that underpins the tariffs on Canada.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Legislation aimed at protecting Florida state parks stuck in committee
item.description: What all started with a leaked proposal to build golf courses and pickleball courts at state parks last year has now resulted in legislation that aims to protect natural grounds from those very acts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump admin withholds millions from Planned Parenthood for civil rights and executive order violations: report
item.description: The Trump administration has reportedly withheld millions of dollars from Planned Parenthood affiliates for violating civil rights laws and executive orders against DEI programs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge postpones DHS’s attempt to end Temporary Protected Status for Venezuelans
item.description: A federal judge ordered to postpone DHS Secretary Kristi Noem's order to terminate the temporary protected status of hundreds of thousands of Venezuelan migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House committee considering a cap on school voucher spending, increases to public school funding
item.description: The House Public Education Committee released new proposals for creating education savings accounts and determining how much state funds public districts get. But a scheduled meeting about the bills was canceled.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump: ‘We'll never stop looking' for American journalist kidnapped in Syria, Austin Tice
item.description: President Donald Trump said that although there has been “virtually no sign" of Tice, his administration would continue to secure Tice’s release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump admin reviewing billions in government contracts, grants for Harvard amid antisemitism allegations
item.description: The Department of Education announced a "comprehensive review" of federal contracts and grants for Harvard University as part of an effort to combat antisemitism on campuses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A DOGE employee is put in charge of the US Institute of Peace, a court filing alleges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An official allied with Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency has been put in charge of the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank targeted by President Donald Trump for closure, according to a federal court filing Monday.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota insurance costs could continue to rise under new Trump tariffs
item.description: Tariffs are taxes on goods imported from other countries, but these new taxes are not just impacting the prices of our T-shirts and televisions. Tariffs may even lead to higher insurance rates here at home.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann and Gretchen Brown, MPR News

item.title: Trump wants new Iran nuclear deal after he withdrew from the first
item.description: Trump withdrew the U.S from the deal during his first term, claiming it was “the worst and most one-sided transaction.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth orders fitness standards to be gender neutral for combat jobs. Many already are
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has ordered the military to make fitness standards for all combat jobs gender neutral, formalizing a process that largely exists for many of those jobs already.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nonprofit groups and Democrats sue Trump administration over election executive order
item.description: President Donald Trump's executive order seeking to overhaul the nation's elections is facing its first legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: EPA chief Zeldin says he is closing the agency's one-room museum, saving taxpayers $600,000 a year
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin says he is closing a one-room museum at the agency's Washington headquarters, saving taxpayers $600,000 a year in operating costs.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP leader: Trump 'messing with' opponents with third term talk
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) on Monday said he believes that President Trump is "messing with" his opponents and the media with his remarks about the possibility of seeking a third term in 2028 despite the 22nd Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court race draws national attention, record out-of-state money
item.description: Zac Schultz, a reporter with PBS Wisconsin’s “Here & Now” program, has been closely covering the race and joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition with the highlights.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: The US is not ready for a nuclear showdown with China, key conservatives warn Trump
item.description: The United States is ill-prepared for a potential nuclear showdown with China, key thinkers with the Heritage Foundation are warning President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's promised 'Liberation Day' of tariffs is coming. Here's what it could mean for you
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says Wednesday will be “Liberation Day" — a moment when he plans to roll out a set of tariffs that he promises will free the United States from foreign goods. The details of Trump's next round of import taxes are still sketchy.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 60 percent disapprove of Trump tariffs: Survey
item.description: Just 38 percent of respondents said they approve of Trump's approach to trade negotiations with other countries.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, NewsNation

item.title: Suspected Tren de Aragua, MS-13 members sent to El Salvador
item.description: Suspected Venezuelan gang members and criminals were among the 17 migrants sent to El Salvador by the U.S. military.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic base's anger puts some party leaders on shaky ground
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The Democratic base is angry. Not just at President Donald Trump, Elon Musk and the “Make America Great Again” movement. Rank-and-file Democrats are mad at their own leaders and increasingly agitating to replace them. Democrats in Arizona and Georgia pushed out their party chairs.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN J. COOPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gov. DeSantis proposes $1K rebate checks to cover Florida homeowners' property tax
item.description: Gov. Ron DeSantis announced a proposal on Monday that would give Florida homeowners a rebate averaging $1,000 for each homesteaded property in the state.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he's considering ways to serve a third term as president
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Sunday that “I’m not joking” about trying to serve a third term, the clearest indication he is considering ways to breach a constitutional barrier against continuing to lead the country after his second term ends at the beginning of 2029.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: From Seattle to Miami, anti-Musk protesters gather at hundreds of Tesla locations
item.description: At a rally in Los Angeles, 65-year-old Phil Ansell said he wanted to participate because "I want to do everything possible to protect democracy in this country.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bowman, MPR News

item.title: Democrats ramp up town halls in GOP districts
item.description: House Democrats are ramping up their aggressive strategy of conducting town halls in Republican-held districts, vying to exploit the GOP’s advised moratorium on the events to make inroads with frustrated voters, pick up battleground seats, and flip control of the House in next year’s midterms.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Wisconsin AG sues to stop Musk's check handout
item.description: Wisconsin Attorney General Josh Kaul sued Elon Musk on Friday over his proposal to pay two people $1 million for voting in the state's Supreme Court race. “The Wisconsin Department of Justice is committed to ensuring that elections in Wisconsin are safe, secure, free, and fair.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Red Lake Nation leader condemns Trump orders during State of the Band Address
item.description: During the Red Lake Nation State of the Band Address Friday, chair Darrell Seki Jr., condemned President Donald Trump’s recent executive orders, saying they have created a crisis throughout Indian Country.
item.dc:creator: Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Dan Goldman supports reforms to NY's evidence laws
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York State budget deadline is this upcoming Tuesday. One of Gov. Kathy Hochul’s priorities for this session is to reform New York’s evidence laws, which were passed in 2019. The governor’s proposal includes three major provisions.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: The Minnesota Democratic-Farmer-Labor Party will elect a new chair on Saturday
item.description: Two Democrats are vying to lead the state’s DFL Party. Members of the DFL will vote Saturday for a replacement to longtime party chair Ken Martin who left earlier this year to head up the Democratic National Committee.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin reporter gives insight on country’s most expensive judicial race
item.description: More than $90 million has poured into a Wisconsin Supreme Court race that will decide the court’s ideological majority.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Per-pupil funding going up $2 next year unless something changes in Nevada budget
item.description: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo's proposed state budget sets per-pupil funding in 2026 at $9,416, just $2 higher than the current funding level. Going higher than that is "impracticable.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California Assembly Democrats announce plans to leave X over disinformation, hate speech
item.description: California Assembly Democrats announced plans to leave X over disinformation, lack of content moderation and hate speech.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the international students and faculty known to be targeted by ICE
item.description: The Trump administration’s crackdown on foreign students and faculty who have voiced support for the Palestinian cause has escalated, with universities across the country seeing students either arrested by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or having their visas revoked.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles Dodgers to make Trump White House visit next month
item.description: The Los Angeles Dodgers plan to visit President Donald Trump at the White House to celebrate their World Series victory, the team announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5
